item.title: The Republicans Who Impugn Talarico’s Manhood
item.description: Attacks on the Democratic Senate candidate in Texas show the GOP’s narrow, anxious definition of masculinity.
item.dc:creator: Helen Lewis

item.title: Yet More Damning Revelations About Graham Platner
item.description: Voters may be questioning the Senate candidate’s claims that he is a changed man.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Bruenig

item.title: The Alligator Alcatraz Boondoggle
item.description: The immigrant-detention facility, which may soon be shut down, has been a cruel and costly publicity stunt.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schlosser

item.title: Why Reading Is Now Restless
item.description: AI has already changed writing. Now the technology is changing what it means to read.
item.dc:creator: Walt Hunter

item.title: A Gift From the Basketball Gods
item.description: The Knicks represent the best of New York sports.
item.dc:creator: Michael Powell

item.title: Donald Trump’s Superficiality Is Bone-Deep
item.description: For the shallowest man ever to occupy the presidency, surface appeal is a guiding principle.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Another Chance for Trump to Cash Out
item.description: The president’s battle over presidential records is more serious than it looks.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: The Left Needs to Rediscover Its Patriotism
item.description: A left that rejects a hopeful, empathetic love of the United States can never win the country to its side.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kazin

item.title: AI Has Ruined the Job Market
item.description: Maybe flawed people were better than brute algorithms.
item.dc:creator: Annie Lowrey

item.title: Trump’s Strange Choice for Director of National Intelligence
item.description: The president’s selection of Bill Pulte is both baffling and predictable.
item.dc:creator: David Frum
